content.text:
<user_query>
Discovery recall fix — implementation spec
Prerequisite: @trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0 or later (resume_session support). Pin in runtime config and rebuild the execution image if it still ships an older CLI.

Problem
Discovery runs can produce incomplete surface inventories and under-specified capability cards:

Surface gaps: User-observable write paths (dialogs, forms, mutation APIs) are sometimes omitted while read-only pages and GET routes are captured.
Depth gaps: Capabilities are sometimes rated below high confidence because only entry-point files were read, not full implementation chains.
Downstream steps only see what earlier artifacts contain. Missing surfaces and shallow reads propagate silently into the review packet and rebuild roadmap.

Approach
Keep the existing generate steps unchanged. Add two verification steps with fixed follow-up questions — not open-ended "review your work" prompts.

1. New step: surfaces-verify
Position: After surfaces, before capability-sketch.

- id: surfaces-verify
  name: Reconcile surfaces census against repo
  requires: [surfaces]
  resume_session: surfaces
  produces:
    - artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md
Idempotency: Skip only when artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md exists.

Exact follow-up questions
The step prompt must ask these questions verbatim, in order. The agent answers each in artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md under a matching heading, then updates artifacts/surfaces.json if any answer reveals a missing surface.

Q1 — Unmapped paths

You just completed the surfaces census. List every directory or file group under source/ that you inspected or considered relevant but did not map to an entry in artifacts/surfaces.json. For each item, state in one sentence why it is not a user-observable surface. If your reason is "implementation detail of surface X", name surface X. If you cannot name a parent surface, treat it as a candidate gap.

Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces

For every surface in artifacts/surfaces.json that reads, lists, searches, or displays domain data (pages, tables, APIs returning collections or records): how is that data created, updated, or deleted in this application? Name the file(s) and UI or API entry point for each write path. If a write path exists but is not in surfaces.json, add it now with kind, evidence_locator, trigger, and observed_outcome. If no write path exists, state that explicitly and cite the evidence.

Q3 — Declared entry points

From route definitions, API route files, cron/schedule configuration, edge-function manifests, package scripts, and container entrypoints in source/: list every declared runtime entry point. For each, either (a) point to the matching surface.id in artifacts/surfaces.json, or (b) explain why it is out of scope for user-observable discovery. Any entry point with neither a match nor a defensible out-of-scope reason is a gap — add the surface or record it as an open gap in the audit log.

Q4 — Non-URL behaviors

List every user-observable behavior in this application that is not reached by navigating to a stable URL (modals, drawers, dialogs, context menus, in-page panels, toast actions, keyboard shortcuts, background jobs with user-visible effects). For each, confirm it appears in artifacts/surfaces.json with the correct kind. Add any that are missing.

Q5 — Correction pass

Based on your answers to Q1–Q4: did you add any surfaces to artifacts/surfaces.json? If yes, list each new surface.id and the question that exposed it. If no, state explicitly: "No surfaces added; all gaps resolved as defensible omissions" and name the single highest-risk omission you upheld and why a rebuild team should accept it.

After answering Q1–Q5, re-validate artifacts/surfaces.json against surfaces.schema.json. Commit surfaces-audit-log.md and any surfaces.json updates.

Downstream change: capability-sketch requires [surfaces-verify].

2. New step: capability-redeepdive
Position: After triangulate, before review-pack. No resume_session.

Trigger: Any capability card with "confidence": "medium" or "low".

Exact follow-up questions
Ask these per triggered capability, in artifacts/redeepdive-log.md under a ## <capability.id> heading. Update the capability JSON and OPEN-QUESTIONS.md after each block.

Q1 — Evidence completeness

For capability <id>: list every file referenced in evidence[]. Did you read each file beyond its entry point (imports, helpers, and callees included)? For each file, answer: fully read / entry point only / not read. If any file is "entry point only" or "not read", read it now before continuing.

Q2 — Internal logic

For capability <id>: what business logic runs between the surface entry point and the observed outcome (validation rules, calculations, authorization checks, error handling, retries)? Cite file:line for each rule you can now state from code. If you still cannot state a rule from code, record it as an open question with the exact file path that must be read.

Q3 — Confidence upgrade

For capability <id>: given what you read in Q1–Q2, should confidence remain <current> or change to high? If upgrading to high, add at least one new scenario grounded in code you had not previously cited. If staying below high, name the single largest remaining evidence gap in one sentence.

Q4 — Open questions

For capability <id>: review OPEN-QUESTIONS.md for questions linked to this capability. For each: resolved (cite new evidence), narrowed (state what is now known and what remains unknown), or unchanged (state why the gap persists). Update OPEN-QUESTIONS.md accordingly.

Idempotency: Skip only when artifacts/redeepdive-log.md exists.

Downstream change: review-pack requires capability-redeepdive.

3. Runtime
Item	Action
Flow CLI
Pin @trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0 in FLOW_NPM_PACKAGE
Image
Rebuild if the running image predates the pin
Validation
Confirm workflow passes flow step validation
4. Acceptance criteria
surfaces-verify uses resume_session: surfaces and the five surface questions appear verbatim in the step prompt.
capability-redeepdive uses the four capability questions verbatim, repeated per non-high-confidence card.
surfaces-audit-log.md and redeepdive-log.md use the question headings as document structure.
Known write-path surfaces appear in surfaces.json after verify on a representative repo.
Each new step is idempotent via its own output artifact.
5. Change surface
workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml — two new steps with the exact prompts above, two requires updates.
No schema changes required for v1.
</user_query>

I'll implement the Discovery recall fix: inspect the workflow and runtime config, then add the two verification steps per your spec.

[REDACTED]

input.command: flow --version 2>/dev/null || echo "flow not installed"; npm list -g @trilogy-group/flow 2>/dev/null || true
test -f /home/vscode/.npmrc && echo "npmrc exists" || echo "no npmrc"; npx -y @trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0 --help 2>&1 | head -30

input.description: Check flow step and run help for validation

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && npx -y @trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0 run --executor fake workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml source_repo=https://github.com/example/repo.git 2>&1 | head -50
input.description: Try fake executor workflow run for validation
Find flow package in npx cache

npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/coverage.schema.json

  - id: capability-redeepdive
    name: Deep-read evidence for non-high-confidence capabilities
    requires: [triangulate]
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      If artifacts/redeepdive-log.md already exists in this run, stop and do nothing further.

      Read artifacts/capabilities-manifest.txt and every capability JSON it lists.
      For each capability card where "confidence" is "medium" or "low", answer the
      four questions below in artifacts/redeepdive-log.md under a ## <capability.id>
      heading. After each capability block, update that capability JSON and
      artifacts/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md as directed. Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      If no capability has "confidence" of "medium" or "low", write artifacts/redeepdive-log.md
      with a single line: "No redeepdive targets; all capabilities are high confidence."
      and stop.

      For each triggered capability (confidence "medium" or "low"), answer under
      ## <capability.id> using these headings and questions verbatim (substitute
      <id> with the capability id and <current> with its current confidence value):

      ## Q1 — Evidence completeness

      For capability <id>: list every file referenced in evidence[]. Did you read each file beyond its entry point (imports, helpers, and callees included)? For each file, answer: fully read / entry point only / not read. If any file is "entry point only" or "not read", read it now before continuing.

      ## Q2 — Internal logic

      For capability <id>: what business logic runs between the surface entry point and the observed outcome (validation rules, calculations, authorization checks, error handling, retries)? Cite file:line for each rule you can now state from code. If you still cannot state a rule from code, record it as an open question with the exact file path that must be read.

      ## Q3 — Confidence upgrade

      For capability <id>: given what you read in Q1–Q2, should confidence remain <current> or change to high? If upgrading to high, add at least one new scenario grounded in code you had not previously cited. If staying below high, name the single largest remaining evidence gap in one sentence.

      ## Q4 — Open questions

      For capability <id>: review OPEN-QUESTIONS.md for questions linked to this capability. For each: resolved (cite new evidence), narrowed (state what is now known and what remains unknown), or unchanged (state why the gap persists). Update OPEN-QUESTIONS.md accordingly.
    produces:
      - artifacts/redeepdive-log.md
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/redeepdive-log.md

  - id: review-pack
    name: Generate user-friendly discovery review packet
    requires: [capability-redeepdive]

content.text:
Validating the workflow with Flow 1.8.0 and adding a unit test for the new steps.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
cd /workspaces/agno && python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from agents.step_max_budget import apply_step_max_budget_usd
src = Path('workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml').read_text()
Path('/tmp/discovery-workflow-validated.yaml').write_text(apply_step_max_budget_usd(src))
print('written')
" && npx -y @trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0 run --executor fake /tmp/discovery-workflow-validated.yaml source_repo=https://github.com/example/repo.git 2>&1 | head -20

input.contents:
"""Workflow pack invariants for discovery recall verification steps."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

_WORKFLOW = Path("workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml")


def test_discovery_recall_steps_present() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    for step_id in (
        "surfaces",
        "surfaces-verify",
        "capability-sketch",
        "triangulate",
        "capability-redeepdive",
        "review-pack",
    ):
        assert f"id: {step_id}" in text

    assert "requires: [surfaces-verify]" in text
    assert "requires: [capability-redeepdive]" in text
    assert "resume_session: surfaces" in text
    assert "artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md" in text
    assert "artifacts/redeepdive-log.md" in text


def test_surfaces_verify_questions_verbatim() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "## Q1 — Unmapped paths" in text
    assert "## Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces" in text
    assert "## Q3 — Declared entry points" in text
    assert "## Q4 — Non-URL behaviors" in text
    assert "## Q5 — Correction pass" in text
    assert (
        'If no, state explicitly: "No surfaces added; all gaps resolved as defensible omissions"'
        in text
    )


def test_capability_redeepdive_questions_verbatim() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "## Q1 — Evidence completeness" in text
    assert "## Q2 — Internal logic" in text
    assert "## Q3 — Confidence upgrade" in text
    assert "## Q4 — Open questions" in text
    assert '"confidence" is "medium" or "low"' in text
    assert "## <capability.id>" in text

input.description: Direct flow validateWorkflowDefinition on discovery YAML

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && uv run ruff check tests/workflows/test_discovery_workflow_paths.py && uv run pytest tests/workflows/ -q
input.description: Lint and run all workflow tests

content.text:
Discovery recall verification is in place. Summary of what changed:

### Workflow (`workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml`)

**`surfaces-verify`** (after `surfaces`, before `capability-sketch`):
- `requires: [surfaces]`, `resume_session: surfaces`
- Idempotent on `artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md`
- Prompt includes Q1–Q5 verbatim with matching `##` headings
- Verify checks audit log exists and `surfaces.json` still validates

**`capability-redeepdive`** (after `triangulate`, before `review-pack`):
- No `resume_session`
- Triggers on capabilities with `"confidence": "medium"` or `"low"`
- Q1–Q4 verbatim, repeated per triggered capability under `## <capability.id>`
- Idempotent on `artifacts/redeepdive-log.md`
- Short-circuit when all capabilities are high confidence

**Dependency updates:**
- `capability-sketch` → `requires: [surfaces-verify]`
- `review-pack` → `requires: [capability-redeepdive]`

### Runtime pin (`@trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0`)

- `Dockerfile` — default `ARG FLOW_NPM_PACKAGE`
- `compose.yaml` — build arg default
- `example.env` — documented override

### Validation

- Flow 1.8.0 accepts the updated workflow (including `resume_session: surfaces`)
- Added `tests/workflows/test_discovery_workflow_paths.py` — 3 tests for step presence, verbatim questions, and dependency wiring
- All 9 workflow tests pass

### Next step for you

Rebuild the API image so the container picks up Flow 1.8.0:

```sh
docker compose build agentos-api
```

Or set `FLOW_NPM_PACKAGE=@trilogy-group/flow@1.8.0` in `.env` if you override the build arg locally.

[REDACTED]

Splitting `surfaces-verify` into two steps (Q1–Q2, then Q3–Q5) and updating the test file.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
- id: surfaces-verify
    name: Reconcile surfaces census — unmapped paths and write paths
    requires: [surfaces]
    resume_session: surfaces
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      If artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md already exists and includes completed answers under
      ## Q1 — Unmapped paths and ## Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces, stop and do nothing further.

      You just completed the surfaces census. Create or update artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md
      with answers under matching headings (use the question title as the heading). Update
      artifacts/surfaces.json if any answer reveals a missing surface. Re-validate
      artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      ## Q1 — Unmapped paths

      You just completed the surfaces census. List every directory or file group under source/ that you inspected or considered relevant but did not map to an entry in artifacts/surfaces.json. For each item, state in one sentence why it is not a user-observable surface. If your reason is "implementation detail of surface X", name surface X. If you cannot name a parent surface, treat it as a candidate gap.

      ## Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces

      For every surface in artifacts/surfaces.json that reads, lists, searches, or displays domain data (pages, tables, APIs returning collections or records): how is that data created, updated, or deleted in this application? Name the file(s) and UI or API entry point for each write path. If a write path exists but is not in surfaces.json, add it now with kind, evidence_locator, trigger, and observed_outcome. If no write path exists, state that explicitly and cite the evidence.
    produces:
      - artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md &&
        grep -q "## Q1 — Unmapped paths" artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md &&
        grep -q "## Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces" artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md &&
        npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json

  - id: surfaces-verify-2
    name: Reconcile surfaces census — entry points, non-URL behaviors, correction
    requires: [surfaces-verify]
    resume_session: surfaces-verify
    max_budget_usd: __STEP_MAX_BUDGET_USD__
    prompt: |
      If artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md already exists and includes a completed answer under
      ## Q5 — Correction pass, stop and do nothing further.

      Read artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md (Q1–Q2 from the prior step). Append answers for
      Q3–Q5 under matching headings (use the question title as the heading). Update
      artifacts/surfaces.json if any answer reveals a missing surface. After answering Q3–Q5,
      re-validate artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      ## Q3 — Declared entry points

      From route definitions, API route files, cron/schedule configuration, edge-function manifests, package scripts, and container entrypoints in source/: list every declared runtime entry point. For each, either (a) point to the matching surface.id in artifacts/surfaces.json, or (b) explain why it is out of scope for user-observable discovery. Any entry point with neither a match nor a defensible out-of-scope reason is a gap — add the surface or record it as an open gap in the audit log.

      ## Q4 — Non-URL behaviors

      List every user-observable behavior in this application that is not reached by navigating to a stable URL (modals, drawers, dialogs, context menus, in-page panels, toast actions, keyboard shortcuts, background jobs with user-visible effects). For each, confirm it appears in artifacts/surfaces.json with the correct kind. Add any that are missing.

      ## Q5 — Correction pass

      Based on your answers to Q1–Q4: did you add any surfaces to artifacts/surfaces.json? If yes, list each new surface.id and the question that exposed it. If no, state explicitly: "No surfaces added; all gaps resolved as defensible omissions" and name the single highest-risk omission you upheld and why a rebuild team should accept it.
    produces:
      - artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md &&
        grep -q "## Q5 — Correction pass" artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md &&
        npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json

  - id: capability-sketch
    name: Cluster surfaces into capability candidates
    requires: [surfaces-verify-2]

content.text:
Split `surfaces-verify` into two steps:

### `surfaces-verify` (Q1–Q2)
- `resume_session: surfaces`
- Questions: **Unmapped paths**, **Write paths for read surfaces**
- Idempotent when Q1 and Q2 are already answered in the audit log
- Verify: audit log has Q1 + Q2 headings, `surfaces.json` validates

### `surfaces-verify-2` (Q3–Q5)
- `requires: [surfaces-verify]`, `resume_session: surfaces-verify`
- Questions: **Declared entry points**, **Non-URL behaviors**, **Correction pass**
- Appends to the same `artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md` from step 1
- Idempotent when Q5 is complete

input.contents:
"""Workflow pack invariants for the Discovery workflow.

These tests prove the YAML-level contract introduced by issue #203:
  - publish_branch and artifacts_root are declared as params.
  - No step prompt contains prose git-commit/push instructions.

Recall verification steps (surfaces-verify, capability-redeepdive) are covered
by the tests in the second half of this module.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

_WORKFLOW = Path("workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml")


def test_discovery_workflow_declares_publish_branch_param() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "publish_branch:" in text, (
        "workflow.yaml must declare publish_branch in params; "
        "the harness refuses to start a run without it"
    )


def test_discovery_workflow_declares_artifacts_root_param() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "artifacts_root:" in text, (
        "workflow.yaml must declare artifacts_root in params; "
        "the harness refuses to start a run without it"
    )


def test_discovery_workflow_publish_branch_default_is_rebase_discovery() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "publish_branch: 'rebase-discovery'" in text


def test_discovery_workflow_artifacts_root_default_is_canonical_path() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "artifacts_root: '.rebaseai/discovery/artifacts'" in text


def test_discovery_step_prompts_have_no_commit_and_push_instruction() -> None:
    """No step prompt may tell the LLM to commit and push."""
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "commit and push" not in text, (
        "Step prompts must not instruct the LLM to commit and push; "
        "the publish guard handles all git operations"
    )


def test_discovery_step_prompts_have_no_copy_to_source_instruction() -> None:
    """No step prompt may tell the LLM to copy files into source/."""
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "Copy new artifacts to source" not in text, (
        "Step prompts must not instruct the LLM to copy files into source/; "
        "the publish guard owns all git-tree mutations"
    )


def test_discovery_verify_blocks_call_publish_guard() -> None:
    """Every step's verify block must invoke the publish guard."""
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    count = text.count("agents.discovery.publish_guard")
    assert count >= 12, (
        f"Expected agents.discovery.publish_guard in all 12 step verify blocks, "
        f"found {count} occurrences; every step must be fail-closed"
    )


def test_discovery_workflow_publish_guard_uses_publish_branch_param() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "--publish-branch {{publish_branch}}" in text


def test_discovery_workflow_publish_guard_uses_artifacts_root_param() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "--artifacts-root {{artifacts_root}}" in text


def test_discovery_all_steps_present() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    for step_id in (
        "acquire",
        "surfaces",
        "surfaces-verify",
        "surfaces-verify-2",
        "capability-sketch",
        "style-extract",
        "capability-deepdive",
        "triangulate",
        "capability-redeepdive",
        "review-pack",
        "roadmap-propose",
        "publish-review-artifacts",
    ):
        assert f"id: {step_id}" in text, f"step '{step_id}' missing from workflow.yaml"


def test_discovery_recall_steps_wiring() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "requires: [surfaces-verify-2]" in text
    assert "requires: [capability-redeepdive]" in text
    assert "resume_session: surfaces" in text
    assert "resume_session: surfaces-verify" in text
    assert "artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md" in text
    assert "artifacts/redeepdive-log.md" in text


def test_surfaces_verify_questions_verbatim() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "## Q1 — Unmapped paths" in text
    assert "## Q2 — Write paths for read surfaces" in text
    assert "## Q3 — Declared entry points" in text
    assert "## Q4 — Non-URL behaviors" in text
    assert "## Q5 — Correction pass" in text
    assert (
        'If no, state explicitly: "No surfaces added; all gaps resolved as defensible omissions"'
        in text
    )


def test_capability_redeepdive_questions_verbatim() -> None:
    text = _WORKFLOW.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "## Q1 — Evidence completeness" in text
    assert "## Q2 — Internal logic" in text
    assert "## Q3 — Confidence upgrade" in text
    assert "## Q4 — Open questions" in text
    assert '"confidence" is "medium" or "low"' in text
    assert "## <capability.id>" in text

input.old_string:
artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      ## Q1 — Unmapped paths
input.new_string:
artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Do NOT perform any git operations (commit, push, or copy files to source/).
      The publish step handles all git work automatically after your artifacts are validated.

      ## Q1 — Unmapped paths

input.old_string:
npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json

  - id: surfaces-verify-2
input.new_string:
npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json &&
        python3 -m agents.discovery.publish_guard
        --step-id surfaces-verify
        --publish-branch {{publish_branch}}
        --artifacts-root {{artifacts_root}}
        --source-dir source
        --produced artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md artifacts/surfaces.json
        --provenance artifacts/publish-provenance.jsonl

  - id: surfaces-verify-2

input.old_string:
re-validate artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      ## Q3 — Declared entry points
input.new_string:
re-validate artifacts/surfaces.json against {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json.
      Do NOT perform any git operations (commit, push, or copy files to source/).
      The publish step handles all git work automatically after your artifacts are validated.

      ## Q3 — Declared entry points

input.old_string:
npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json

  - id: capability-sketch
input.new_string:
npx -y ajv-cli validate -s {{workflow_dir}}/schemas/surfaces.schema.json
        -d artifacts/surfaces.json &&
        python3 -m agents.discovery.publish_guard
        --step-id surfaces-verify-2
        --publish-branch {{publish_branch}}
        --artifacts-root {{artifacts_root}}
        --source-dir source
        --produced artifacts/surfaces-audit-log.md artifacts/surfaces.json
        --provenance artifacts/publish-provenance.jsonl

  - id: capability-sketch

input.old_string:
artifacts/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md as directed. Copy new artifacts to source, commit and push.

      If no capability has "confidence" of "medium" or "low", write artifacts/redeepdive-log.md
input.new_string:
artifacts/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md as directed.
      Do NOT perform any git operations (commit, push, or copy files to source/).
      The publish step handles all git work automatically after your artifacts are validated.

      If no capability has "confidence" of "medium" or "low", write artifacts/redeepdive-log.md

input.old_string:
verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/redeepdive-log.md

  - id: review-pack
input.new_string:
verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/redeepdive-log.md &&
        python3 -m agents.discovery.publish_guard
        --step-id capability-redeepdive
        --publish-branch {{publish_branch}}
        --artifacts-root {{artifacts_root}}
        --source-dir source
        --produced artifacts/redeepdive-log.md artifacts/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md
        --from-manifest artifacts/capabilities-manifest.txt
        --provenance artifacts/publish-provenance.jsonl

  - id: review-pack

content.text:
Done.

1. **Committed** your local discovery workflow changes (pull would have failed otherwise).
2. **`git pull --rebase origin main`** — rebased onto `bde3c73` (publish-guard / issue #203 work).
3. **Resolved conflicts** in `tests/workflows/test_discovery_workflow_paths.py` by merging both test suites, and aligned the new verify steps with origin’s publish-guard pattern (removed `commit and push` from prompts, added `publish_guard` to verify blocks).
4. **`git push origin main`** — `main` is now at `311808b` on origin.

All 13 discovery workflow tests pass.
